# Clawperator

Clawperator is a deterministic Android automation runtime for AI agents. It is the actuator layer: your external agent or LLM owns reasoning and planning, and Clawperator executes validated Android actions and returns structured results.

Clawperator operates an Android device on behalf of a user. That Android device can be:

- a physical Android burner device connected over adb
- a local Android emulator provisioned through the Node CLI

## Start here

- Homepage: https://clawperator.com/
- For agents: https://clawperator.com/agents
- Markdown index: https://clawperator.com/index.md
- Docs: https://docs.clawperator.com/
- Agent quickstart: https://docs.clawperator.com/ai-agents/agent-quickstart/
- Agent guide: https://docs.clawperator.com/ai-agents/node-api-for-agents/
- Snapshot format: https://docs.clawperator.com/reference/snapshot-format/
- Operator LLM playbook: https://docs.clawperator.com/design/operator-llm-playbook/
- API overview: https://docs.clawperator.com/reference/api-overview/
- CLI reference: https://docs.clawperator.com/reference/cli-reference/
- Full compiled docs: https://clawperator.com/llms-full.txt
- GitHub: https://github.com/clawperator/clawperator

## Notes for agents

- The docs site contains the canonical technical documentation.
- Prefer the docs site, Node API guide, and CLI reference over inferring behavior from marketing copy.
- Clawperator is an actuator, not an autonomous planner.
- A physical Android burner device is the preferred actuator environment for compatibility and long-running reliability.
- If no physical Android device is available, install Clawperator first, then provision an emulator with `clawperator provision emulator`.
- Clawperator is a neutral actuator. The external agent decides the workflow, and Clawperator provides the UI interaction primitives.
- Skills repository: https://github.com/clawperator/clawperator-skills
- Install skills: run `clawperator skills install` after setup
- Skills registry: `~/.clawperator/skills/skills/skills-registry.json` (set via CLAWPERATOR_SKILLS_REGISTRY)
- Skill scripts are authored in Node.js; invoke with: `node ~/.clawperator/skills/skills/<id>/scripts/<name>.js <deviceId> [args]`
